name: Alabama
incomeTaxRates: 2% to 5% graduated state income tax
payFrequencyRules: No broad statewide pay frequency rule; use a consistent published schedule.
suiRate: Experience-rated; new employer rate assigned by Alabama Department of Labor
localMinimumWage: No broad local minimum wage variations.
payrollTax: Withhold Alabama income tax and remit employer unemployment contributions.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to the Alabama New-Hire Reporting Program within 7 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with five or more employees.
paidLeave: No statewide paid family leave program; federal FMLA may apply.
finalPayRules: No special statewide deadline; pay by the next regular payday.
uniqueRules: Some local occupational taxes can apply depending on work location.

name: Alaska
incomeTaxRates: No state individual income tax
payFrequencyRules: Employees must be paid at least monthly; many employers use semi-monthly schedules.
suiRate: Employer and employee unemployment contributions apply; rates vary by experience.
keyAgencies: Alaska Department of Labor and Workforce Development
localMinimumWage: No broad local minimum wage variations.
payrollTax: No state income tax withholding; unemployment contributions are the core state payroll tax.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to the Alaska New Hire Reporting Center within 20 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with one or more employees.
paidLeave: Paid sick leave requirements begin in 2025 under Alaska law; track accrual and usage carefully.
finalPayRules: Discharged employees generally must be paid within three working days; resignations by next regular payday.
uniqueRules: Alaska has employee-side unemployment withholding in addition to employer unemployment tax.

name: Arizona
incomeTaxRates: Flat 2.5% state income tax
payFrequencyRules: Most employees must be paid at least twice per month, no more than 16 days apart.
suiRate: Experience-rated; new employer rate assigned by Arizona DES
localMinimumWage: Flagstaff and some localities may require higher minimum wages.
payrollTax: Withhold Arizona income tax and remit employer unemployment contributions.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to Arizona New Hire Reporting within 20 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with one or more employees.
paidLeave: Earned paid sick time applies statewide under Arizona law.
finalPayRules: Terminated employees generally due wages within seven working days or next payday, whichever is sooner.
uniqueRules: Arizona withholding elections use state-specific percentage options.

name: Arkansas
incomeTaxRates: Graduated state income tax with top rate around 3.9% in 2025
payFrequencyRules: Most employees must be paid at least semi-monthly.
suiRate: Experience-rated; new employer rate assigned by Arkansas Division of Workforce Services
keyAgencies: Arkansas Department of Finance and Administration
Arkansas Department of Labor and Licensing
localMinimumWage: No broad local minimum wage variations.
payrollTax: Withhold Arkansas income tax and remit employer unemployment contributions.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to the Arkansas New Hire Reporting Center within 20 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with three or more employees.
paidLeave: No statewide paid family leave mandate; federal FMLA may apply.
finalPayRules: Discharged employees generally must be paid by the next regular payday.
uniqueRules: Some wage payment rules differ for railroad and public service employers.

name: California
incomeTaxRates: 1% to 12.3% graduated income tax plus additional high-earner tax where applicable
minimumWage: $16.50/hour statewide in 2025, with many higher local rates
payFrequencyRules: Most wages must be paid at least twice monthly on designated paydays.
suiRate: Experience-rated unemployment tax plus ETT, SDI, and PIT withholding obligations.
localMinimumWage: Many cities and counties have higher minimum wages, including Los Angeles, San Francisco, San Jose, and others.
payrollTax: Withhold PIT and SDI; remit UI, ETT, and employment training taxes where applicable.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to California EDD within 20 days.
workersComp: Required for employers with one or more employees.
paidLeave: State paid sick leave, paid family leave, disability insurance, and local leave rules may apply.
finalPayRules: Terminated employees generally due final wages immediately; resignations generally within 72 hours unless notice given.
uniqueRules: Daily overtime, meal/rest breaks, wage statements, and local ordinances make California payroll unusually complex.

name: Colorado
incomeTaxRates: Flat 4.4% state income tax
payFrequencyRules: Employees generally must be paid at least monthly or every 30 days, whichever is longer.
suiRate: Experience-rated; new employer rate assigned by Colorado Department of Labor and Employment
keyAgencies: Colorado Department of Labor and Employment
localMinimumWage: Denver, Boulder County, and some localities may require higher minimum wages.
payrollTax: Withhold Colorado income tax; remit unemployment and paid family leave premiums.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to the Colorado State Directory of New Hires within 20 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with one or more employees.
paidLeave: Colorado FAMLI paid family and medical leave premiums and HFWA paid sick leave apply.
finalPayRules: Discharged employees are generally due final wages immediately or within statutory timing limits.
uniqueRules: Colorado has detailed wage statement, vacation payout, paid leave, and equal pay notice obligations.

name: Connecticut
incomeTaxRates: Graduated state income tax from 2% to 6.99%
payFrequencyRules: Wages are generally due weekly unless the labor department approves a different schedule.
suiRate: Experience-rated unemployment tax; paid leave contributions are employee funded.
localMinimumWage: No broad local minimum wage variations.
payrollTax: Withhold Connecticut income tax and paid leave contributions; remit unemployment contributions.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to Connecticut Directory of New Hires within 20 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with one or more employees.
paidLeave: Paid sick leave and Connecticut Paid Leave requirements may apply.
finalPayRules: Discharged employees generally due final wages by the next business day.
uniqueRules: Connecticut has paid leave payroll deductions and industry-specific sick leave coverage rules.

name: Delaware
incomeTaxRates: Graduated state income tax from 2.2% to 6.6%
payFrequencyRules: Employees generally must be paid at least monthly.
suiRate: Experience-rated; new employer rate assigned by Delaware Division of Unemployment Insurance
localMinimumWage: No broad local minimum wage variations.
payrollTax: Withhold Delaware income tax; remit unemployment and training tax where applicable.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to the Delaware State Directory of New Hires within 20 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with one or more employees.
paidLeave: Delaware paid family and medical leave payroll contributions phase in before benefits begin.
finalPayRules: Final wages are generally due by the next regular payday.
uniqueRules: Employers may need to track local Wilmington earned income tax exposure separately.

name: Florida
incomeTaxRates: No state individual income tax
minimumWage: $13.00/hour through September 29, 2025, then scheduled to increase
payFrequencyRules: No broad statewide pay frequency rule; use a consistent published schedule.
suiRate: Reemployment tax is experience-rated; new employer rate assigned by Florida Department of Revenue
localMinimumWage: No broad local minimum wage variations for private employers.
payrollTax: No state income tax withholding; remit Florida reemployment tax.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to the Florida New Hire Reporting Center within 20 days.
workersComp: Generally required for non-construction employers with four or more employees and construction employers with one or more.
paidLeave: No statewide paid family leave mandate; federal FMLA may apply.
finalPayRules: No special statewide deadline; pay by the next regular payday.
uniqueRules: Florida minimum wage changes annually under its constitutional schedule.

name: Georgia
incomeTaxRates: Flat state income tax, scheduled around 5.39% in 2025
minimumWage: $7.25/hour federal minimum wage for covered employees
payFrequencyRules: Most employees must be paid at least semi-monthly.
suiRate: Experience-rated; new employer rate assigned by Georgia Department of Labor
keyAgencies: Georgia State Board of Workers' Compensation
localMinimumWage: No broad local minimum wage variations.
payrollTax: Withhold Georgia income tax and remit employer unemployment contributions.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to Georgia New Hire Reporting within 10 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with three or more employees.
paidLeave: No statewide paid family leave mandate for private employers; federal FMLA may apply.
finalPayRules: No special statewide deadline; pay by the next regular payday.
uniqueRules: Georgia requires state withholding account registration before payroll withholding begins.

name: Hawaii
incomeTaxRates: Graduated state income tax from 1.4% to 11%
payFrequencyRules: Employees generally must be paid at least twice monthly unless an exception applies.
suiRate: Experience-rated unemployment contributions; temporary disability insurance requirements also apply.
keyAgencies: Hawaii Department of Labor and Industrial Relations
localMinimumWage: No broad local minimum wage variations.
payrollTax: Withhold Hawaii income tax; remit unemployment and required disability-related contributions.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to Hawaii New Hire Reporting within 20 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with one or more employees.
paidLeave: Temporary disability insurance is required; paid sick leave is not broadly statewide for private employers.
finalPayRules: Discharged employees generally due final wages immediately or by the next working day depending on circumstances.
uniqueRules: Hawaii has prepaid health care and temporary disability insurance payroll compliance requirements.

name: Idaho
incomeTaxRates: Flat state income tax around 5.695%
payFrequencyRules: Employees generally must be paid at least once per month.
suiRate: Experience-rated; new employer rate assigned by Idaho Department of Labor
localMinimumWage: No broad local minimum wage variations.
payrollTax: Withhold Idaho income tax and remit employer unemployment contributions.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to Idaho New Hire Reporting within 20 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with one or more employees.
paidLeave: No statewide paid family leave mandate; federal FMLA may apply.
finalPayRules: Final wages generally due by the next regular payday or within 10 days, whichever is sooner.
uniqueRules: Idaho wage claims and final pay timing can accelerate if an employee makes a written request.

name: Illinois
incomeTaxRates: Flat 4.95% state income tax
payFrequencyRules: Most employees must be paid at least semi-monthly; some workers weekly.
suiRate: Experience-rated; new employer rate assigned by Illinois Department of Employment Security
localMinimumWage: Chicago and Cook County may require higher local minimum wages and leave rules.
payrollTax: Withhold Illinois income tax and remit employer unemployment contributions.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to the Illinois New Hire Directory within 20 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with one or more employees.
paidLeave: Illinois Paid Leave for All Workers Act and local paid sick leave rules may apply.
finalPayRules: Final wages generally due by the next regular payday.
uniqueRules: Illinois requires reimbursement of necessary employee expenses and detailed wage payment compliance.

name: Indiana
incomeTaxRates: Flat state income tax plus county income tax where applicable
payFrequencyRules: Employees generally must be paid at least semi-monthly or biweekly.
suiRate: Experience-rated; new employer rate assigned by Indiana Department of Workforce Development
localMinimumWage: No broad local minimum wage variations.
payrollTax: Withhold Indiana state and county income tax; remit unemployment contributions.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to the Indiana New Hire Reporting Center within 20 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with one or more employees.
paidLeave: No statewide paid family leave mandate; federal FMLA may apply.
finalPayRules: Final wages generally due by the next regular payday.
uniqueRules: Indiana county income tax makes employee work and residence location setup important.

name: Iowa
incomeTaxRates: Graduated state income tax transitioning toward a flat structure
payFrequencyRules: Employees generally must be paid at least monthly, no later than 12 days after the pay period.
suiRate: Experience-rated; new employer rate assigned by Iowa Workforce Development
localMinimumWage: No broad local minimum wage variations.
payrollTax: Withhold Iowa income tax and remit employer unemployment contributions.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to Iowa Centralized Employee Registry within 15 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with one or more employees.
paidLeave: No statewide paid family leave mandate; federal FMLA may apply.
finalPayRules: Final wages generally due by the next regular payday.
uniqueRules: Iowa requires careful tracking of wage deduction authorizations.

name: Kansas
incomeTaxRates: Graduated state income tax from 3.1% to 5.7%
payFrequencyRules: Employees generally must be paid at least monthly.
suiRate: Experience-rated; new employer rate assigned by Kansas Department of Labor
localMinimumWage: No broad local minimum wage variations.
payrollTax: Withhold Kansas income tax and remit employer unemployment contributions.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to the Kansas New Hire Directory within 20 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with payroll above statutory thresholds.
paidLeave: No statewide paid family leave mandate; federal FMLA may apply.
finalPayRules: Final wages generally due by the next regular payday.
uniqueRules: Kansas withholding and unemployment accounts are separate registrations.

name: Kentucky
incomeTaxRates: Flat state income tax around 4% in 2025
payFrequencyRules: Employees generally must be paid at least semi-monthly.
suiRate: Experience-rated; new employer rate assigned by Kentucky Office of Unemployment Insurance
localMinimumWage: No broad local minimum wage variations, but local occupational taxes are common.
payrollTax: Withhold Kentucky income tax and local occupational taxes where applicable; remit unemployment contributions.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to Kentucky New Hire Reporting within 20 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with one or more employees.
paidLeave: No statewide paid family leave mandate; federal FMLA may apply.
finalPayRules: Final wages generally due by the next regular payday or within 14 days, whichever is later.
uniqueRules: Kentucky local occupational license taxes can materially affect payroll setup.

name: Louisiana
incomeTaxRates: Graduated state income tax from 1.85% to 4.25%
payFrequencyRules: Employers must designate regular paydays; some industries have semi-monthly rules.
suiRate: Experience-rated; new employer rate assigned by Louisiana Workforce Commission
localMinimumWage: No broad local minimum wage variations.
payrollTax: Withhold Louisiana income tax and remit employer unemployment contributions.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to Louisiana Directory of New Hires within 20 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with one or more employees.
paidLeave: No statewide paid family leave mandate; federal FMLA may apply.
finalPayRules: Final wages generally due by the next regular payday or within 15 days, whichever occurs first.
uniqueRules: Louisiana requires clear wage payment policies for commissions, bonuses, and deductions.

name: Maine
incomeTaxRates: Graduated state income tax from 5.8% to 7.15%
payFrequencyRules: Employees generally must be paid at regular intervals not exceeding 16 days.
suiRate: Experience-rated; new employer rate assigned by Maine Department of Labor
localMinimumWage: Portland and Rockland may have higher local wage rules.
payrollTax: Withhold Maine income tax and remit employer unemployment contributions.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to Maine New Hire Reporting within 7 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with one or more employees.
paidLeave: Paid leave contributions under Maine's paid family and medical leave program phase in before benefits begin.
finalPayRules: Final wages generally due by the next regular payday or within two weeks after demand.
uniqueRules: Maine has earned paid leave and developing paid family and medical leave payroll obligations.

name: Maryland
incomeTaxRates: Graduated state income tax plus county income tax
payFrequencyRules: Employees generally must be paid at least every two weeks or twice per month.
suiRate: Experience-rated; new employer rate assigned by Maryland Division of Unemployment Insurance
localMinimumWage: Montgomery County and some localities may have higher minimum wage requirements.
payrollTax: Withhold Maryland state and local income taxes; remit unemployment contributions.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to the Maryland State Directory of New Hires within 20 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with one or more employees.
paidLeave: Maryland paid sick and safe leave applies; paid family and medical leave contributions are scheduled under state law.
finalPayRules: Final wages generally due by the next regular payday.
uniqueRules: County tax withholding makes employee county setup important.

name: Massachusetts
incomeTaxRates: Flat 5% income tax plus 4% surtax on high income above the state threshold
payFrequencyRules: Most hourly employees must be paid weekly or biweekly; salaried employees may be semi-monthly.
suiRate: Experience-rated unemployment tax plus paid family and medical leave contributions.
keyAgencies: Massachusetts Department of Family and Medical Leave
localMinimumWage: No broad local minimum wage variations.
payrollTax: Withhold Massachusetts income tax and PFML contributions; remit unemployment contributions.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to Massachusetts within 14 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with one or more employees.
paidLeave: Earned sick time and Paid Family and Medical Leave apply statewide.
finalPayRules: Discharged employees generally due wages on the day of discharge; resignations by next regular payday.
uniqueRules: Massachusetts has strict wage act liability for late wages and vacation payout.

name: Michigan
incomeTaxRates: Flat state income tax around 4.25% plus local city income taxes where applicable
minimumWage: $10.56/hour in early 2025 with scheduled changes under state law
payFrequencyRules: Employees generally must be paid at least monthly; many categories have semi-monthly requirements.
suiRate: Experience-rated; new employer rate assigned by Michigan Unemployment Insurance Agency
keyAgencies: Michigan Department of Labor and Economic Opportunity
localMinimumWage: No broad local minimum wage variations, but some city income taxes apply.
payrollTax: Withhold Michigan and applicable city income tax; remit unemployment contributions.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to Michigan New Hire Operations Center within 20 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with three or more employees or one employee working 35+ hours per week.
paidLeave: Michigan earned sick time requirements changed under state law; review current coverage rules.
finalPayRules: Final wages generally due by the next regular payday.
uniqueRules: City income tax withholding can apply for Detroit and other Michigan cities.

name: Minnesota
incomeTaxRates: Graduated state income tax from 5.35% to 9.85%
payFrequencyRules: Employees generally must be paid at least once every 31 days; commissions at least every three months.
suiRate: Experience-rated; new employer rate assigned by Minnesota Unemployment Insurance Program
keyAgencies: Minnesota Department of Labor and Industry
localMinimumWage: Minneapolis and St. Paul may require higher local minimum wages.
payrollTax: Withhold Minnesota income tax and remit unemployment contributions.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to the Minnesota New Hire Reporting Center within 20 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with one or more employees.
paidLeave: Earned sick and safe time applies; paid family and medical leave contributions phase in under state law.
finalPayRules: Discharged employees can demand final wages within 24 hours; resignations generally by next payday.
uniqueRules: Minnesota has local sick leave history and statewide earned sick and safe time coordination.

name: Mississippi
incomeTaxRates: Flat 4.7% state income tax in 2025 after lower bracket phaseout
payFrequencyRules: No broad statewide pay frequency rule for most private employers.
suiRate: Experience-rated; new employer rate assigned by Mississippi Department of Employment Security
localMinimumWage: No broad local minimum wage variations.
payrollTax: Withhold Mississippi income tax and remit employer unemployment contributions.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to Mississippi State Directory of New Hires within 15 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with five or more employees.
paidLeave: No statewide paid family leave mandate; federal FMLA may apply.
finalPayRules: No special statewide deadline; pay by the next regular payday.
uniqueRules: Mississippi payroll compliance is simpler than many states but still requires tax and UI registration.

name: Missouri
incomeTaxRates: Graduated state income tax with top rate around 4.7%
payFrequencyRules: Most employees must be paid at least semi-monthly.
suiRate: Experience-rated; new employer rate assigned by Missouri Division of Employment Security
localMinimumWage: No broad local minimum wage variations for private employers; local earnings taxes may apply.
payrollTax: Withhold Missouri income tax and local earnings tax where applicable; remit unemployment contributions.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to Missouri Directory of New Hires within 20 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with five or more employees; construction has stricter rules.
paidLeave: Missouri paid sick leave rules are scheduled under voter-approved law; review current effective dates.
finalPayRules: Discharged employees generally must be paid on the day of discharge.
uniqueRules: St. Louis and Kansas City earnings taxes can affect payroll withholding.

name: Montana
incomeTaxRates: State income tax with reduced bracket structure and top rate around 5.9%
payFrequencyRules: Employers must pay within 10 business days after wages are due unless exceptions apply.
suiRate: Experience-rated; new employer rate assigned by Montana Department of Labor and Industry
keyAgencies: Montana Department of Labor and Industry
localMinimumWage: No broad local minimum wage variations.
payrollTax: Withhold Montana income tax and remit unemployment contributions.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to Montana New Hire Reporting within 20 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with one or more employees.
paidLeave: No statewide paid family leave mandate; federal FMLA may apply.
finalPayRules: Discharged employees generally due final wages immediately unless employer has a written policy extending timing within legal limits.
uniqueRules: Montana wage payment and wrongful discharge rules can affect termination workflows.

name: Nebraska
incomeTaxRates: Graduated state income tax with top rate around 5.2% in 2025
payFrequencyRules: Employers must designate regular paydays.
suiRate: Experience-rated; new employer rate assigned by Nebraska Department of Labor
localMinimumWage: No broad local minimum wage variations.
payrollTax: Withhold Nebraska income tax and remit unemployment contributions.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to Nebraska New Hire Reporting within 20 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with one or more employees.
paidLeave: No statewide paid family leave mandate; federal FMLA may apply.
finalPayRules: Final wages generally due by the next regular payday or within two weeks, whichever is sooner.
uniqueRules: Nebraska minimum wage is scheduled to increase annually under voter-approved law.

name: Nevada
incomeTaxRates: No state individual income tax
payFrequencyRules: Employees generally must be paid at least semi-monthly.
suiRate: Experience-rated unemployment tax plus Commerce Tax considerations for some employers.
localMinimumWage: No broad local minimum wage variations.
payrollTax: No state income tax withholding; remit unemployment contributions and monitor Modified Business Tax exposure.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to Nevada New Hire Reporting Center within 20 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with one or more employees.
paidLeave: Paid leave applies to many private employers with 50 or more employees.
finalPayRules: Discharged employees generally due final wages immediately; resignations generally within seven days or next payday.
uniqueRules: Nevada Modified Business Tax can function like a payroll-based business tax.

name: New Hampshire
incomeTaxRates: No broad wage income tax
payFrequencyRules: Employees generally must be paid at least weekly unless approved for a different schedule.
suiRate: Experience-rated; new employer rate assigned by New Hampshire Employment Security
keyAgencies: New Hampshire Department of Revenue Administration
localMinimumWage: No broad local minimum wage variations.
payrollTax: No wage income tax withholding; remit unemployment contributions.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to New Hampshire Employment Security within 20 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with one or more employees.
paidLeave: Voluntary paid family and medical leave program options exist; federal FMLA may apply.
finalPayRules: Discharged employees generally due final wages within 72 hours; resignations by next regular payday.
uniqueRules: New Hampshire focuses payroll compliance on wage payment, unemployment, and workers comp rather than income tax withholding.

name: New Jersey
incomeTaxRates: Graduated state income tax from 1.4% to 10.75%
payFrequencyRules: Employees generally must be paid at least twice per month; executives may be monthly.
suiRate: Employer and employee unemployment, disability, and family leave contributions may apply.
keyAgencies: New Jersey Department of Labor and Workforce Development
localMinimumWage: No broad local minimum wage variations.
payrollTax: Withhold New Jersey income tax plus employee UI, disability, and family leave contributions where applicable.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to New Jersey New Hire Directory within 20 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with one or more employees.
paidLeave: Earned sick leave, temporary disability insurance, and family leave insurance apply statewide.
finalPayRules: Final wages generally due by the next regular payday.
uniqueRules: New Jersey has multiple employee payroll deductions beyond income tax.

name: New Mexico
incomeTaxRates: Graduated state income tax from 1.7% to 5.9%
payFrequencyRules: Employees generally must be paid at least semi-monthly.
suiRate: Experience-rated; new employer rate assigned by New Mexico Department of Workforce Solutions
keyAgencies: New Mexico Taxation and Revenue Department
New Mexico Department of Workforce Solutions
localMinimumWage: Albuquerque, Santa Fe, and Bernalillo County may have higher local rules.
payrollTax: Withhold New Mexico income tax and remit unemployment contributions.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to New Mexico New Hire Directory within 20 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with three or more employees; construction has broader coverage.
paidLeave: Healthy Workplaces Act paid sick leave applies statewide.
finalPayRules: Discharged employees generally due final wages within five days for fixed wages; other compensation has separate timing.
uniqueRules: Local wage ordinances and statewide paid sick leave require location-aware payroll setup.

name: New York
incomeTaxRates: Graduated state income tax from 4% to 10.9%, plus local NYC/Yonkers tax where applicable
minimumWage: $15.50 statewide and $16.50 in NYC, Long Island, and Westchester in 2025
payFrequencyRules: Manual workers generally weekly; clerical and other workers at least semi-monthly.
suiRate: Experience-rated unemployment tax plus paid family leave and disability insurance requirements.
keyAgencies: New York Department of Taxation and Finance
localMinimumWage: NYC, Long Island, and Westchester have higher minimum wage rates.
payrollTax: Withhold state and applicable local income tax; administer PFL and disability deductions where applicable.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to New York New Hire Reporting within 20 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with one or more employees.
paidLeave: Paid sick leave, Paid Family Leave, and disability benefits apply statewide.
finalPayRules: Final wages generally due by the next regular payday.
uniqueRules: New York has wage notice, wage statement, spread-of-hours, local tax, disability, and paid family leave complexity.

name: North Carolina
incomeTaxRates: Flat state income tax around 4.25% in 2025
payFrequencyRules: Employers must notify employees of regular paydays and pay on those dates.
suiRate: Experience-rated; new employer rate assigned by North Carolina Division of Employment Security
keyAgencies: North Carolina Division of Employment Security
localMinimumWage: No broad local minimum wage variations for private employers.
payrollTax: Withhold North Carolina income tax and remit unemployment contributions.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to North Carolina New Hire Directory within 20 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with three or more employees.
paidLeave: No statewide paid family leave mandate for private employers; federal FMLA may apply.
finalPayRules: Final wages generally due by the next regular payday.
uniqueRules: North Carolina requires advance notice for changes to promised wages.

name: North Dakota
incomeTaxRates: Low graduated state income tax with top rate under 3%
payFrequencyRules: Employees generally must be paid at least monthly.
suiRate: Experience-rated; new employer rate assigned by Job Service North Dakota
keyAgencies: North Dakota Office of State Tax Commissioner
North Dakota Workforce Safety and Insurance
localMinimumWage: No broad local minimum wage variations.
payrollTax: Withhold North Dakota income tax and remit unemployment contributions.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to North Dakota New Hire Reporting within 20 days.
workersComp: Coverage is generally obtained through North Dakota Workforce Safety and Insurance.
paidLeave: No statewide paid family leave mandate; federal FMLA may apply.
finalPayRules: Final wages generally due by the next regular payday.
uniqueRules: North Dakota has a monopolistic state workers compensation system.

name: Ohio
incomeTaxRates: Graduated state income tax plus municipal and school district taxes where applicable
payFrequencyRules: Employees generally must be paid at least semi-monthly.
suiRate: Experience-rated; new employer rate assigned by Ohio Department of Job and Family Services
keyAgencies: Ohio Department of Job and Family Services
localMinimumWage: No broad local minimum wage variations, but municipal income taxes are common.
payrollTax: Withhold Ohio state, school district, and municipal taxes where applicable; remit unemployment contributions.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to Ohio New Hire Reporting Center within 20 days.
workersComp: Coverage is generally obtained through Ohio Bureau of Workers' Compensation.
paidLeave: No statewide paid family leave mandate for private employers; federal FMLA may apply.
finalPayRules: Final wages generally due by the next regular payday or within 15 days, whichever is earlier.
uniqueRules: Ohio municipal and school district tax withholding can be the hardest part of payroll setup.

name: Oklahoma
incomeTaxRates: Graduated state income tax with top rate around 4.75%
payFrequencyRules: Employees generally must be paid at least semi-monthly.
suiRate: Experience-rated; new employer rate assigned by Oklahoma Employment Security Commission
localMinimumWage: No broad local minimum wage variations.
payrollTax: Withhold Oklahoma income tax and remit unemployment contributions.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to Oklahoma New Hire Reporting Center within 20 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with one or more employees unless an exception applies.
paidLeave: No statewide paid family leave mandate; federal FMLA may apply.
finalPayRules: Final wages generally due by the next regular payday.
uniqueRules: Oklahoma unemployment rates may include technology or conditional assessments.

name: Oregon
incomeTaxRates: Graduated state income tax from 4.75% to 9.9%
minimumWage: Regional minimum wage rates apply and adjust annually
payFrequencyRules: Employees generally must be paid at least monthly; many employers pay semi-monthly.
suiRate: Experience-rated unemployment tax; statewide transit and paid leave contributions may apply.
keyAgencies: Oregon Bureau of Labor and Industries
localMinimumWage: Portland metro, standard, and nonurban rates differ under Oregon law.
payrollTax: Withhold Oregon income tax; remit unemployment, Paid Leave Oregon, and applicable transit taxes.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to Oregon Department of Justice Division of Child Support within 20 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with one or more employees.
paidLeave: Paid Leave Oregon and paid sick time apply statewide.
finalPayRules: Discharged employees generally due final wages by the end of the next business day; resignation timing varies by notice.
uniqueRules: Oregon payroll can include transit district taxes and regional minimum wage rates.

name: Pennsylvania
incomeTaxRates: Flat 3.07% state income tax plus local earned income tax where applicable
payFrequencyRules: Employees generally must be paid on regular paydays designated in advance.
suiRate: Employer unemployment is experience-rated; employee unemployment withholding also applies.
keyAgencies: Pennsylvania Department of Labor and Industry
localMinimumWage: No broad local minimum wage variations, but local earned income taxes are widespread.
payrollTax: Withhold Pennsylvania state tax, local EIT, local services tax where applicable, and employee unemployment contributions.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to Pennsylvania New Hire Reporting Program within 20 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with one or more employees.
paidLeave: No broad statewide paid sick leave, but Philadelphia, Pittsburgh, and Allegheny County local rules may apply.
finalPayRules: Final wages generally due by the next regular payday.
uniqueRules: Pennsylvania local tax withholding requires PSD codes and work/residence location tracking.

name: Rhode Island
incomeTaxRates: Graduated state income tax from 3.75% to 5.99%
payFrequencyRules: Most employees must be paid weekly unless an exception applies.
suiRate: Experience-rated unemployment tax; temporary disability and caregiver insurance contributions apply.
keyAgencies: Rhode Island Department of Labor and Training
localMinimumWage: No broad local minimum wage variations.
payrollTax: Withhold Rhode Island income tax and employee TDI/TCI contributions; remit unemployment contributions.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to Rhode Island New Hire Reporting within 14 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with one or more employees.
paidLeave: Paid sick and safe leave plus temporary caregiver insurance may apply.
finalPayRules: Final wages generally due by the next regular payday.
uniqueRules: Rhode Island has employee temporary disability and caregiver insurance payroll deductions.

name: South Carolina
incomeTaxRates: Graduated state income tax with top rate around 6.2%
payFrequencyRules: Employers must notify employees of wage payment terms and pay accordingly.
suiRate: Experience-rated; new employer rate assigned by South Carolina Department of Employment and Workforce
keyAgencies: South Carolina Department of Employment and Workforce
localMinimumWage: No broad local minimum wage variations.
payrollTax: Withhold South Carolina income tax and remit unemployment contributions.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to South Carolina State Directory of New Hires within 20 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with four or more employees.
paidLeave: No statewide paid family leave mandate; federal FMLA may apply.
finalPayRules: Final wages generally due within 48 hours or by the next regular payday, not exceeding 30 days.
uniqueRules: South Carolina unemployment taxes may include contingency or solvency adjustments.

name: South Dakota
incomeTaxRates: No state individual income tax
payFrequencyRules: Employees generally must be paid at least monthly.
suiRate: Experience-rated; new employer rate assigned by South Dakota Department of Labor and Regulation
keyAgencies: South Dakota Department of Labor and Regulation
localMinimumWage: No broad local minimum wage variations.
payrollTax: No state income tax withholding; remit unemployment contributions.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to South Dakota New Hire Reporting Center within 20 days.
workersComp: Workers compensation coverage is strongly encouraged and may be required by contract, though rules differ from many states.
paidLeave: No statewide paid family leave mandate; federal FMLA may apply.
finalPayRules: Final wages generally due by the next regular payday or when the employee returns employer property.
uniqueRules: South Dakota has no state income tax but still requires UI and wage payment compliance.

name: Tennessee
incomeTaxRates: No state individual wage income tax
payFrequencyRules: Private employers generally must pay at least semi-monthly.
suiRate: Experience-rated; new employer rate assigned by Tennessee Department of Labor and Workforce Development
keyAgencies: Tennessee Department of Labor and Workforce Development
localMinimumWage: No broad local minimum wage variations.
payrollTax: No state income tax withholding; remit unemployment contributions.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to Tennessee New Hire Reporting Program within 20 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with five or more employees; construction has stricter rules.
paidLeave: No statewide paid family leave mandate; federal FMLA may apply.
finalPayRules: Final wages generally due by the next regular payday or within 21 days, whichever is later.
uniqueRules: Tennessee has no wage income tax, but unemployment and workers comp setup still matters.

name: Texas
incomeTaxRates: No state individual income tax
payFrequencyRules: Non-exempt employees generally must be paid at least twice monthly; exempt employees at least monthly.
suiRate: Experience-rated; new employer rate assigned by Texas Workforce Commission
localMinimumWage: No broad local minimum wage variations for private employers.
payrollTax: No state income tax withholding; remit unemployment contributions.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to the Texas Employer New Hire Reporting Program within 20 days.
workersComp: Private employers can generally opt out, but non-subscriber rules and notice requirements apply.
paidLeave: No statewide paid family leave mandate; federal FMLA may apply.
finalPayRules: Discharged employees generally due final wages within six calendar days; resignations by next regular payday.
uniqueRules: Texas pay frequency and final pay rules are central because there is no state income tax withholding.

name: Utah
incomeTaxRates: Flat state income tax around 4.55%
payFrequencyRules: Employees generally must be paid at least semi-monthly.
suiRate: Experience-rated; new employer rate assigned by Utah Department of Workforce Services
localMinimumWage: No broad local minimum wage variations.
payrollTax: Withhold Utah income tax and remit unemployment contributions.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to Utah New Hire Registry within 20 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with one or more employees.
paidLeave: No statewide paid family leave mandate for private employers; federal FMLA may apply.
finalPayRules: Discharged employees generally due final wages within 24 hours; resignations by next regular payday.
uniqueRules: Utah has strict timing for involuntary termination final pay.

name: Vermont
incomeTaxRates: Graduated state income tax from 3.35% to 8.75%
payFrequencyRules: Employees generally must be paid weekly unless notice is given for biweekly or semi-monthly pay.
suiRate: Experience-rated; new employer rate assigned by Vermont Department of Labor
localMinimumWage: No broad local minimum wage variations.
payrollTax: Withhold Vermont income tax and remit unemployment contributions.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to Vermont New Hire Reporting within 10 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with one or more employees.
paidLeave: Paid sick leave applies statewide; voluntary paid family leave options exist.
finalPayRules: Discharged employees generally due final wages within 72 hours; resignations by next regular payday.
uniqueRules: Vermont paid sick leave and final pay timing require clear termination workflows.

name: Virginia
incomeTaxRates: Graduated state income tax from 2% to 5.75%
payFrequencyRules: Hourly employees generally must be paid at least every two weeks or twice per month; salaried employees at least monthly.
suiRate: Experience-rated; new employer rate assigned by Virginia Employment Commission
keyAgencies: Virginia Department of Labor and Industry
localMinimumWage: No broad local minimum wage variations.
payrollTax: Withhold Virginia income tax and remit unemployment contributions.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to Virginia New Hire Reporting Center within 20 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with more than two employees.
paidLeave: Limited paid sick leave applies to certain home health workers; federal FMLA may apply.
finalPayRules: Final wages generally due by the next regular payday.
uniqueRules: Virginia minimum wage has scheduled changes and coverage rules that should be reviewed annually.

name: Washington
incomeTaxRates: No state wage income tax
payFrequencyRules: Employees generally must be paid at least monthly on regular paydays.
suiRate: Experience-rated unemployment tax; paid family leave and workers comp premiums apply.
keyAgencies: Washington Department of Labor and Industries
localMinimumWage: Seattle, SeaTac, Tukwila, and other localities may require higher minimum wages.
payrollTax: No state wage income tax; remit unemployment, Paid Family and Medical Leave, WA Cares where applicable, and workers comp premiums.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to Washington Division of Child Support within 20 days.
workersComp: Coverage is generally through Washington Department of Labor and Industries.
paidLeave: Paid sick leave, Paid Family and Medical Leave, and WA Cares payroll deductions may apply.
finalPayRules: Final wages generally due by the next regular payday.
uniqueRules: Washington has no income tax, but multiple payroll premiums and local wage rules make setup complex.

name: West Virginia
incomeTaxRates: Graduated state income tax with reduced rates in 2025
payFrequencyRules: Employees generally must be paid at least twice every month.
suiRate: Experience-rated; new employer rate assigned by WorkForce West Virginia
keyAgencies: West Virginia Offices of the Insurance Commissioner
localMinimumWage: No broad local minimum wage variations.
payrollTax: Withhold West Virginia income tax and remit unemployment contributions.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to West Virginia New Hire Reporting Center within 14 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with one or more employees.
paidLeave: No statewide paid family leave mandate; federal FMLA may apply.
finalPayRules: Final wages generally due by the next regular payday.
uniqueRules: West Virginia rate reductions and wage payment rules should be reviewed before each tax year.

name: Wisconsin
incomeTaxRates: Graduated state income tax from 3.5% to 7.65%
payFrequencyRules: Most employees must be paid at least monthly, with no more than 31 days between paydays.
suiRate: Experience-rated; new employer rate assigned by Wisconsin Department of Workforce Development
localMinimumWage: No broad local minimum wage variations.
payrollTax: Withhold Wisconsin income tax and remit unemployment contributions.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to Wisconsin New Hire Reporting Center within 20 days.
workersComp: Generally required for employers with three or more employees or payroll above statutory thresholds.
paidLeave: No statewide paid family leave mandate; federal FMLA may apply.
finalPayRules: Final wages generally due by the next regular payday.
uniqueRules: Wisconsin requires close attention to wage deductions and payroll recordkeeping.

name: Wyoming
incomeTaxRates: No state individual income tax
minimumWage: $7.25/hour federal minimum wage for covered employees
payFrequencyRules: Employees generally must be paid at least semi-monthly.
suiRate: Experience-rated; new employer rate assigned by Wyoming Department of Workforce Services
localMinimumWage: No broad local minimum wage variations.
payrollTax: No state income tax withholding; remit unemployment and workers compensation contributions where applicable.
newHireReporting: Report new hires to Wyoming New Hire Reporting Center within 20 days.
workersComp: Wyoming has state-administered workers compensation requirements for covered employments.
paidLeave: No statewide paid family leave mandate; federal FMLA may apply.
finalPayRules: Final wages generally due by the next regular payday.
uniqueRules: Wyoming payroll setup focuses on unemployment, workers compensation, and industry classification.
